# Add an intent + benefit layer to the cloud-deployment guides

Parent epic: #12225

## Context

The `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/` guides are **mechanics-heavy and intent-light**. The Overview opens with *"what cloud-native KB ingestion means"* → contract splits, `parsed-chunk-v1`, topology anchors. A reader learns *how* to wire ingestion but never *why* to deploy the Agent OS, *what* they get, or the recommended end-to-end path. They also over-index on Knowledge Base ingestion, which is only a fraction of the Brain.

This sub is part of the #12225 organism-apex rollout: it adds the benefit/intent layer for deploying the Brain without changing runtime behavior or naming clients.

## The Fix

Add the missing why/benefit/path layer (capability framing, fully generic — no client names):

- A "why deploy the Agent OS" overview: what a deployed Brain gives you (a memory-backed, cross-family engineering team operating over your own content), framed as capability/benefit.
- The recommended end-to-end deployment path, top-down, before the contract-level mechanics.
- Rebalance: present the full Brain (Memory Core, orchestrator, dream/golden-path, A2A) — not KB ingestion alone — as what is being deployed.
- Keep the existing mechanics guides; this adds the layer above them.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A why/benefit/intent overview exists ahead of the mechanics
- [ ] A clear recommended end-to-end deployment path is documented
- [ ] Coverage rebalanced from KB-ingestion-centric to whole-Brain
- [ ] No client/partner/customer names; capability framing only
- [ ] Cross-family review per ADR 0018 §2.6

## Out of Scope

- Monetization, pricing, commercial terms
- Any specific client deployment
- Engine/runtime changes

## Related

- Parent: #12225 · Surfaces: `learn/agentos/cloud-deployment/**`
